transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: Good morning. Last standup before UAT. Let's go around quickly. Varsha — async queue?

transcript.speaker: Varsha
transcript.text: Done. Redis came up at 2:15pm yesterday as Vivek promised. I switched from mock immediately, finished the consumer logic and retry scheduler by 9pm, deployed to prod at 10:30pm. I ran manual end-to-end tests this morning — payment forward, payment failure, refund initiation. All three flows are going through the queue correctly.

transcript.speaker: Vivek
transcript.text: Redis is stable, no issues overnight. I've also added a monitoring alert — if queue size crosses 100 pending items I get a ping immediately. So we'll know before it becomes a problem.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: Ajay — test suite?

transcript.speaker: Ajay
transcript.text: I ran the full 34 test cases on prod this morning. All 34 passing. Including — and I want to say this clearly — the 2 intermittent cases that were failing since last Friday. They are both passing consistently now. I ran each one 5 times. 5 out of 5 both times. Async queue fixed them.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: Finally. Those 2 have been following us since Day 5 of this sprint.

transcript.speaker: Ajay
transcript.text: Yes. I'm closing them now. With a proper resolution note.

transcript.speaker: Suraj
transcript.text: From yesterday's session with Ajay — I found and fixed 2 more edge cases on my side. Browser back button during payment now shows a proper warning instead of breaking the flow. And the loading spinner was not showing on slow connections — that's fixed too. I tested on iPhone and Android both this morning.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: Good. What about the double-payment scenario Ajay raised in yesterday's session?

transcript.speaker: Ajay
transcript.text: Varsha added idempotency key check last night. I tested it — if same order is submitted twice, the second request returns a clear message instead of creating a duplicate transaction. Works correctly.

transcript.speaker: Varsha
transcript.text: And browser close during active payment — I handled it too. Transaction completes in background via the queue, email confirmation is sent. When user comes back and reloads, the page reconciles to the correct state from the server.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: These are exactly the scenarios the UAT team will throw at us on Monday. Good that we covered them.

transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: I want to say something before we close. This was not an easy sprint. We had environment issues on Day 1, demo failure on Day 3, SSL cert bomb on Day 4, partial go-live on Day 5. A lot went wrong. But the team stayed on it throughout, worked late multiple nights, and we are going into UAT in a stronger position than we were even planning to be at the start of the week. I appreciate the effort.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: Thank you sir. Especially Varsha and Ajay — multiple nights past midnight. That shouldn't be normal but it made the difference.

transcript.speaker: Varsha
transcript.text: Hopefully Sprint 5 has better infra support from the start.

transcript.speaker: Ajay
transcript.text: I'm sending the UAT scenario document to client now. They'll have it 30 minutes before UAT starts Monday morning.

transcript.speaker: Suraj
transcript.text: I'm also sending a short UI walkthrough doc — screen by screen, what to expect. So their testers aren't confused by any flows.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: One important thing for the weekend — if anything comes up during UAT Monday, post in the Sprint 4 internal group first. We discuss, we agree on the response, then we reply to client. No individual panic responses directly on client calls. Agreed?

transcript.speaker: Varsha
transcript.text: Agreed.

transcript.speaker: Ajay
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Suraj
transcript.text: Understood.

transcript.speaker: Vivek
transcript.text: I'm on infra monitoring all weekend. Any prod issue I'll respond immediately.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: One more thing — after UAT, I want to schedule a proper retrospective. Day 1 env delay, Day 4 SSL cert issue, staging vs prod parity — these patterns need to be formally documented so Sprint 5 doesn't repeat them.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: 100%. I'll schedule it for next Tuesday. Lessons learned doc — infra communication, environment parity, what we'd do differently. We close Sprint 4 properly.

transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: Good idea. Schedule it. Alright everyone — good work this sprint. Let's have a clean UAT on Monday.
